rationale: The cited paper appears as a single illustrative citation in the background discussion that non-utility cues can increase variety seeking (shape-array priming). The citing paper does not build its theory, hypotheses, design, or measures on that specific study; its mechanism centers on sleepiness and need for arousal, drawing primarily on other stimulation/arousal work. Because this citation serves as contextual prior evidence rather than a paper-specific building block, it is a passing mention.
